level_signals: 5+ years of technical program management experience
Track record of delivering complex infrastructure programs
Comp range $290K-$365K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic
Expected to partner closely with engineering leadership on strategic initiatives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 5+ years of technical program management experience, with a track record of successfully delivering complex infrastructure programs in ML/AI systems or large-scale distributed systems
Have deep technical understanding of infrastructure systems—enough to engage substantively with engineers, identify technical risks, and add value beyond project tracking
Excel at creating structure and processes in ambiguous environments, bringing clarity to complex cross-team initiatives
Have strong stakeholder management skills and can build trust with both technical and non-technical partners
Are comfortable navigating competing priorities and using data to drive technical decisions
Have experience with developer productivity initiatives, CI/CD systems, or infrastructure scaling
Thrive in fast-paced environments and can balance strategic planning with tactical execution
Are obsessed with reliability, scalability, security, and continuous improvement
Are passionate about AI infrastructure and understand the unique challenges of building and operating systems at frontier scale
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with Kubernetes, cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure), and ML infrastructure (GPU/TPU/Trainium clusters)
Background working with research teams and translating their needs into concrete technical requirements
Experience driving adoption of AI tools to improve engineering productivity
Familiarity with observability tooling and practices
Have a passion for supporting internal partners like research to understand their unique needs

scope.surface: Infrastructure organization — training clusters, production infrastructure, developer platforms, CI/CD, reliability programs
scope.business_model: Internal platform / infrastructure enablement supporting AI research and product
core_responsibilities: Drive cross-functional programs to improve developer environments, CI/CD infrastructure, and release processes
Coordinate large-scale migrations and platform modernization efforts across engineering teams
Partner with teams to measure and improve developer productivity metrics
Lead initiatives to integrate AI tools into development workflows
Drive programs to establish and achieve reliability targets across training infrastructure and production services
Coordinate incident response improvements, post-mortem processes, and on-call rotations
Establish metrics and dashboards to track infrastructure health, capacity utilization, and operational excellence
Serve as the critical bridge between infrastructure teams, research, and product
Consult with stakeholders to understand infrastructure, data, and compute needs
Drive alignment on priorities and timelines across teams with competing constraints

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (implies options/RSUs in package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($290K-$365K base)
Clear scope: infrastructure spanning training, production, and developer platforms
No application deadline — rolling basis reduces urgency pressure
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit mention of diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% in-office time; San Francisco only — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Salary range is very wide ($75K spread), making it difficult to assess where a candidate might land
JD blends must-have and nice-to-have requirements in a single bulleted list without clear delineation — several items could be interpreted as either required or preferred
No explicit team size or reporting structure mentioned

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (only San Francisco mentioned as HQ; no other role-specific cities named)
equity_form (inferred from 'optional equity donation matching' benefit language)
ambiguities: The JD lists requirements under a single 'You May Be a Good Fit If You' section without distinguishing required vs. preferred. The final four bullets (Kubernetes/cloud, research team background, AI tools adoption, observability) read as additional preferred signals but are not explicitly labeled as 'nice to have' or 'preferred'. Treated as nice_to_haves here based on their placement after the core list and their conditional phrasing.
The 'passion for supporting internal partners' bullet could be either a cultural_fit must-have or a soft nice-to-have; classified as nice-to-have given its placement and softer language.

Minimum years of experience in Logistics section states 'Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' — vague; the 5+ years stated in the body is the operative figure used here.
